English: The OGP Values Check is an effort to ensure that new countries joining OGP adhere to the democratic governance norms and values set forth in the Open Government Declaration , and applies to governments wanting to join OGP.
Original_Text: The OGP Values Check is an effort to ensure that new countries joining OGP adhere to the democratic governance norms and values set forth in the Open Government Declaration , and applies to governments wanting to join OGP.

English: Please refer to the Eligibility Criteria page for more details and a breakdown on the specific scoring methodologies used to assess both dimensions of OGP eligibility.
Original_Text: Please refer to the Eligibility Criteria page for more details and a breakdown on the specific scoring methodologies used to assess both dimensions of OGP eligibility.

English: Each government aspiring to join OGP should identify a lead ministry or government agency that will assume the responsibility for coordinating the government’s OGP process and activities and serve as the official contact point for the Partnership.
Original_Text: Each government aspiring to join OGP should identify a lead ministry or government agency that will assume the responsibility for coordinating the government’s OGP process and activities and serve as the official contact point for the Partnership.

English: Each government is expected to designate both a high-level and working-level point of contact (PoC).
Original_Text: Each government is expected to designate both a high-level and working-level point of contact (PoC).

English: The former is normally a ministerial level official who represents the government formally and officially within the Partnership, while the latter is a senior civil servant with ability to coordinate across government and serve as the day-to-day contact point for the Support Unit.
Original_Text: The former is normally a ministerial level official who represents the government formally and officially within the Partnership, while the latter is a senior civil servant with ability to coordinate across government and serve as the day-to-day contact point for the Support Unit.

English: If a country passes the Core Eligibility Criteria and Values Check and the government identified a lead ministry or agency, it should signal its intent to participate in OGP by sending a Letter of Intent, which formally expresses the government’s intention to join OGP.
Original_Text: If a country passes the Core Eligibility Criteria and Values Check and the government identified a lead ministry or agency, it should signal its intent to participate in OGP by sending a Letter of Intent, which formally expresses the government’s intention to join OGP.

English: Letters of Intent should confirm that the government is eligible to join OGP, specifically endorse the Open Government Declaration , describe past open government reforms, and specify the lead ministry or agency and at least the high-level point of contact that will be responsible for OGP within the government.
Original_Text: Letters of Intent should confirm that the government is eligible to join OGP, specifically endorse the Open Government Declaration , describe past open government reforms, and specify the lead ministry or agency and at least the high-level point of contact that will be responsible for OGP within the government.

English: Letters of Intent should be addressed to the OGP Co-Chairs and sent to [email protected] , signed by a ministerial-level official from any ministry or agency within government, as long as that agency has received approval from the Head of State or Government to join OGP.
Original_Text: Letters of Intent should be addressed to the OGP Co-Chairs and sent to [email protected] , signed by a ministerial-level official from any ministry or agency within government, as long as that agency has received approval from the Head of State or Government to join OGP.

English: Governments should ensure that there are paths to becoming active in OGP through the global community, the government’s own OGP dialogue mechanism, or both.
Original_Text: Governments should ensure that there are paths to becoming active in OGP through the global community, the government’s own OGP dialogue mechanism, or both.

English: They are the product of a co-creation process in which government and civil society define ambitious commitments to foster transparency, accountability and inclusion.

Original_Text: They are the product of a co-creation process in which government and civil society define ambitious commitments to foster transparency, accountability and inclusion.

English: Participating governments are encouraged to produce an End-of-term Self-Assessment Report on the final results of reforms completed in the action plan, consultation during implementation, and lessons learned.
Original_Text: Participating governments are encouraged to produce an End-of-term Self-Assessment Report on the final results of reforms completed in the action plan, consultation during implementation, and lessons learned.

English: More to promote more transparent, accountable, responsive and inclusive governance.
Original_Text: More to promote more transparent, accountable, responsive and inclusive governance.

English: Jurisdictions not in OGP Local can participate in their national OGP process, which is regulated by the OGP National Handbook or apply for OGP Local when OGP launches a call for applications.
Original_Text: Jurisdictions not in OGP Local can participate in their national OGP process, which is regulated by the OGP National Handbook or apply for OGP Local when OGP launches a call for applications.

English: In order to become a member, local jurisdictions must meet the eligibility criteria and submit a Letter of Support.
Original_Text: In order to become a member, local jurisdictions must meet the eligibility criteria and submit a Letter of Support.

English: A minimum set of eligibility criteria is required to join OGP Local to ensure adequate conditions for developing and implementing an action plan Action plans are at the core of a government’s participation in OGP.
Original_Text: A minimum set of eligibility criteria is required to join OGP Local to ensure adequate conditions for developing and implementing an action plan Action plans are at the core of a government’s participation in OGP.

English: During open calls for new members, a local government that meets eligibility criteria and wants to join OGP must submit a Letter of Support (LOS).
Original_Text: During open calls for new members, a local government that meets eligibility criteria and wants to join OGP must submit a Letter of Support (LOS).

English: This LOS should be three (3) pages maximum on the local government’s letterhead, in English, supporting the application of their jurisdiction and committing to meeting OGP values and the above criteria.
Original_Text: This LOS should be three (3) pages maximum on the local government’s letterhead, in English, supporting the application of their jurisdiction and committing to meeting OGP values and the above criteria.

English: It must be signed by the head of the local government or a senior local official with delegated authority, and at least one (1) non-governmental stakeholder 1 willing to support and be part of the OGP Local process.
Original_Text: It must be signed by the head of the local government or a senior local official with delegated authority, and at least one (1) non-governmental stakeholder 1 willing to support and be part of the OGP Local process.

English: The Letter of Support must address: The Letters of Support will be assessed by the Support Unit based on the criteria outlined above.
Original_Text: The Letter of Support must address: The Letters of Support will be assessed by the Support Unit based on the criteria outlined above.

English: In addition, other factors will be considered to ensure a diverse cohort, such as regional balance, type of jurisdiction and population size, as well as different strengths to complement the overall objectives of OGP Local.
Original_Text: In addition, other factors will be considered to ensure a diverse cohort, such as regional balance, type of jurisdiction and population size, as well as different strengths to complement the overall objectives of OGP Local.

English: Specific guidance and examples will be provided by the Support Unit at the start of a call for new members.
Original_Text: Specific guidance and examples will be provided by the Support Unit at the start of a call for new members.

English: Each action plan must be co-created by government and non-governmental stakeholders, implemented, monitored and assessed.
Original_Text: Each action plan must be co-created by government and non-governmental stakeholders, implemented, monitored and assessed.

English: OGP refers to the person responsible for coordinating the government’s participation in OGP Local as a Local Government Point of Contact (POC) .
Original_Text: OGP refers to the person responsible for coordinating the government’s participation in OGP Local as a Local Government Point of Contact (POC) .

English: The role is crucial and multidimensional; points of contact are at the forefront of transparency, participation, and accountability efforts for an OGP participating government.
Original_Text: The role is crucial and multidimensional; points of contact are at the forefront of transparency, participation, and accountability efforts for an OGP participating government.

English: The OGP Local Point of Contact responsibilities and activities include: The participation of both government and non-governmental stakeholders is essential to the success of the OGP.
Original_Text: The OGP Local Point of Contact responsibilities and activities include: The participation of both government and non-governmental stakeholders is essential to the success of the OGP.

English: They contribute with the technical expertise, human resources, and convening capacity to effectively ensure the OGP principle of co-creation and participation is fulfilled.
Original_Text: They contribute with the technical expertise, human resources, and convening capacity to effectively ensure the OGP principle of co-creation and participation is fulfilled.

English: For this reason, it must be a trusted and independent actor.
Original_Text: For this reason, it must be a trusted and independent actor.

English: Local monitoring bodies must, at a minimum, use the forms provided by OGP Local to conduct their reporting and are highly encouraged to use the guidance materials provided by OGP Local and the IRM to conduct their work.
Original_Text: Local monitoring bodies must, at a minimum, use the forms provided by OGP Local to conduct their reporting and are highly encouraged to use the guidance materials provided by OGP Local and the IRM to conduct their work.

English: The Support Unit aims to ensure that high-quality direction and information is available to OGP stakeholders, whether directly, through institutional partners, or through peer-to-peer engagement.
Original_Text: The Support Unit aims to ensure that high-quality direction and information is available to OGP stakeholders, whether directly, through institutional partners, or through peer-to-peer engagement.

English: The OGP Local Team will be the main point of contact for the OGP Support Unit for all OGP Local members.
Original_Text: The OGP Local Team will be the main point of contact for the OGP Support Unit for all OGP Local members.

English: The action plan cycle must align with the term limits of the local government in office.
Original_Text: The action plan cycle must align with the term limits of the local government in office.

English: During this period, members should identify how they will use the OGP action plan process to deliver on their strategic, longer-term open government goals and identify the key stakeholders to engage before commencing public consultation activities.
Original_Text: During this period, members should identify how they will use the OGP action plan process to deliver on their strategic, longer-term open government goals and identify the key stakeholders to engage before commencing public consultation activities.

English: The online orientation program must be completed by new OGP Local members as they begin the action plan co-creation process.
Original_Text: The online orientation program must be completed by new OGP Local members as they begin the action plan co-creation process.

English: During this period the member must co-create the new Local Open Government Strategic Vision and initial commitments.
Original_Text: During this period the member must co-create the new Local Open Government Strategic Vision and initial commitments.

English: It should be based on policy principles, seek to achieve long-term outcomes and identify medium-term outputs.
Original_Text: It should be based on policy principles, seek to achieve long-term outcomes and identify medium-term outputs.

English: The Local Open Government Strategic Vision requires an inclusive process to ensure the “buy-in” from key actors both within and outside government.
Original_Text: The Local Open Government Strategic Vision requires an inclusive process to ensure the “buy-in” from key actors both within and outside government.

English: Lastly, it must also include overall information on the activities that will be carried out during this period to monitor and publicize progress, including the designated Monitoring Body.
Original_Text: Lastly, it must also include overall information on the activities that will be carried out during this period to monitor and publicize progress, including the designated Monitoring Body.

English: In either case, OGP Local members should demonstrate clear improvement from the current state of practice.
Original_Text: In either case, OGP Local members should demonstrate clear improvement from the current state of practice.

English: Each commitment must clearly identify the problem that it seeks to address, expected result of the commitment, milestones and implementation dates, available resources and the responsible organizations for the implementation of the commitment.
Original_Text: Each commitment must clearly identify the problem that it seeks to address, expected result of the commitment, milestones and implementation dates, available resources and the responsible organizations for the implementation of the commitment.

English: The plan must contain specific, time-bound, and measurable commitments that are: Commitments should have a minimum duration of one calendar year, but they can also span the entire action plan period.
Original_Text: The plan must contain specific, time-bound, and measurable commitments that are: Commitments should have a minimum duration of one calendar year, but they can also span the entire action plan period.

English: Once a commitment is completely implemented it must be assessed by the Monitoring Body, and a new commitment can be co-created and included in the action plan as long as it can be fully implemented during the action plan period.
Original_Text: Once a commitment is completely implemented it must be assessed by the Monitoring Body, and a new commitment can be co-created and included in the action plan as long as it can be fully implemented during the action plan period.

English: Any changes to a commitment must be consulted and approved with the non-governmental stakeholders involved in the co-creation process.
Original_Text: Any changes to a commitment must be consulted and approved with the non-governmental stakeholders involved in the co-creation process.

English: Any changes, including the process for arriving at the decision, must be documented.
Original_Text: Any changes, including the process for arriving at the decision, must be documented.

English: In the case an OGP Local member wants to make a change to an existing commitment, this must be communicated to the OGP Local Team.
Original_Text: In the case an OGP Local member wants to make a change to an existing commitment, this must be communicated to the OGP Local Team.

Le PGO désigne la personne chargée de coordonner la participation du gouvernement au PGO local comme Point de contact du gouvernement local (PC) .
Le rôle est crucial et multidimensionnel; les points de contact sont à l’avant-garde des efforts de transparence, de participation et de redevabilité pour un gouvernement participant au PGO.
Les responsabilités et les activités du Point de contact du PGO local comprennent : La participation des parties prenantes gouvernementales et non gouvernementales est essentielle au succès du PGO.
L’équipe du PGO local sera le principal point de contact de l’Unité de soutien du PGO pour tous les membres du PGO local.

En plus du dépôt électronique des rapports d’évaluation susmentionnés, le Point de contact local sera tenu de fournir des mises à jour publiques trimestrielles sur l’avancement des engagements par le biais d’un outil de suivi en ligne sur le site du PGO local.
Seulement les points de contact du gouvernement peuvent soumettre la stratégie du plan d’action a travers la plateforme en ligne.
Chaque point de contact va recevoir ses identifiants pour la plateforme en ligne via courriel. 1.1.

English: The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) is a public body established under Section 3 (1) of the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011.
Original_Text: The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) is a public body established under Section 3 (1) of the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011.

English: To combat and prevent corruption, economic crime and unethical conduct in Kenya through law enforcement, prevention, public education, promotion of standards and practices of integrity, ethics and anti-corruption.
Original_Text: To combat and prevent corruption, economic crime and unethical conduct in Kenya through law enforcement, prevention, public education, promotion of standards and practices of integrity, ethics and anti-corruption.

English: To promote integrity and combat corruption through law enforcement, prevention and education.
Original_Text: To promote integrity and combat corruption through law enforcement, prevention and education.

English: The Ethics and Anti- Corruption Commission (EACC) is a statutory body, established under Section 3 of the Ethics and Anti -Corruption Commission Act No. 22 of 2011 pursuant to Article 79 of the Constitution of Kenya.
Original_Text: The Ethics and Anti- Corruption Commission (EACC) is a statutory body, established under Section 3 of the Ethics and Anti -Corruption Commission Act No. 22 of 2011 pursuant to Article 79 of the Constitution of Kenya.

English: The mandate of the Commission is to combat and prevent corruption through: • Preventive measures • Promotion of standards and best practices in integrity and ethics.
Original_Text: The mandate of the Commission is to combat and prevent corruption through: • Preventive measures • Promotion of standards and best practices in integrity and ethics.

English: The mandate of the Commission is derived from the provisions of: • Articles 79 and 252 of the Constitution • The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011 • The Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012.
Original_Text: The mandate of the Commission is derived from the provisions of: • Articles 79 and 252 of the Constitution • The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011 • The Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012.

English: Parliament enacted the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011 and Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012 to provide, inter alia for the functions and powers of the Commission as well as its critical place in the implementation of Chapter Six of the Constitution.
Original_Text: Parliament enacted the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011 and Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012 to provide, inter alia for the functions and powers of the Commission as well as its critical place in the implementation of Chapter Six of the Constitution.

English: The Acts are being implemented alongside the Anti-Corruption and Economics Crimes Act (ACECA) of 2003.
Original_Text: The Acts are being implemented alongside the Anti-Corruption and Economics Crimes Act (ACECA) of 2003.

English: The mandate of the Commission is to combat and prevent corruption through law enforcement, preventive measures, education and promotion of standards and best practices in integrity and ethics.
Original_Text: The mandate of the Commission is to combat and prevent corruption through law enforcement, preventive measures, education and promotion of standards and best practices in integrity and ethics.

English: The mandate of the Commission is derived from the provisions of Articles 79 and 252 of the Constitution, The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011, and the Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012.
Original_Text: The mandate of the Commission is derived from the provisions of Articles 79 and 252 of the Constitution, The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act, 2011, and the Leadership and Integrity Act, 2012.

English: The country’s anti-corruption legislation dates back to 1956 with the enactment of the now defunct Prevention of Corruption Act (formerly Cap. 65, LOK).
Original_Text: The country’s anti-corruption legislation dates back to 1956 with the enactment of the now defunct Prevention of Corruption Act (formerly Cap. 65, LOK).

English: Initially, the Prevention of Corruption Act (Cap. 65) was enforced by the Police Department, notably the Anti-Corruption Squad constituted in 1993.
Original_Text: Initially, the Prevention of Corruption Act (Cap. 65) was enforced by the Police Department, notably the Anti-Corruption Squad constituted in 1993.

English: The Squad was however disbanded in 1995 before it could make any significant impact Amendment of the Prevention of Corruption Act (Cap 65, LOK) in early 1997 led to the creation of the Kenya Anti-Corruption Authority (KACA), whose first director was John Harun Mwau.
Original_Text: The Squad was however disbanded in 1995 before it could make any significant impact Amendment of the Prevention of Corruption Act (Cap 65, LOK) in early 1997 led to the creation of the Kenya Anti-Corruption Authority (KACA), whose first director was John Harun Mwau.

English: The Anti-Corruption Police Unit (ACPU) was created by Executive Order in August 2001.
Original_Text: The Anti-Corruption Police Unit (ACPU) was created by Executive Order in August 2001.

English: ACPU, which operated under the Criminal Investigations Department (CID) of the Police took over KACA’s mantle in September 2001 and performed this function until creation of the Kenya Anti-Corruption Commission (KACC).
Original_Text: ACPU, which operated under the Criminal Investigations Department (CID) of the Police took over KACA’s mantle in September 2001 and performed this function until creation of the Kenya Anti-Corruption Commission (KACC).

English: KACC was a public body established under the Anti-Corruption and Economic Crimes Act (ACECA) No. 3 of 2003 on 2nd May 2003.
Original_Text: KACC was a public body established under the Anti-Corruption and Economic Crimes Act (ACECA) No. 3 of 2003 on 2nd May 2003.

English: Pursuant to Article 79 of the Constitution, Parliament disbanded KACC on 24th August 2011 through enactment of the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act (EACC), 2011.
Original_Text: Pursuant to Article 79 of the Constitution, Parliament disbanded KACC on 24th August 2011 through enactment of the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission Act (EACC), 2011.
